relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: Do not bow to made images; YHWH alone is Divine.
Keep Sabbath and revere the sanctuary.
Walking in the statutes opens the covenant path.
Rain comes in season, and land and trees bear fruit.
Harvest overlaps harvest, and the people eat in security.
Peace fills the land, and fear loosens its grip.
Enemies fall before the people.
Small numbers overcome overwhelming force.
YHWH turns toward the people and establishes covenant.
Old abundance makes room for new.
The dwelling is set in the people's midst.
YHWH walks among them, Divine with a people.
The Liberator breaks the yoke and makes them walk upright.
The warning begins when listening and doing collapse.
Rejecting the statutes is pictured as covenant rupture.
The ancient warning names terror, disease, loss, and enemy consumption.
Fear and defeat intensify under the broken covenant.
The warning escalates sevenfold when correction is refused.
Heaven hardens like iron and earth like bronze.
Labor drains away without fruit.
Contrary walking brings further covenant plague imagery.
Wildness, bereavement, and empty roads enter the warning.
Refusing correction deepens the rupture.
The text mirrors contrary walking with contrary response.
Sword, siege, pestilence, and enemy power are named.
Bread becomes scarce, weighed, and unsatisfying.
The people still refuse to listen.
The warning rises into fury language.
Siege horror reaches the unbearable image of parents consuming children.
Idol places and bodies collapse together.
Cities and sanctuaries become ruin.
The land becomes desolation before enemy eyes.
The people are scattered and the land emptied.
At last the land receives the Sabbaths denied to it.
Desolation becomes forced rest for exploited ground.
Trauma follows the survivors into exile, where even a leaf sounds like pursuit.
They stumble in fear though no pursuer is present.
Exile threatens the people with disappearance.
The remnant wastes under accumulated iniquity.
Confession begins with their own unfaithfulness and that of their ancestors.
The humbled heart finally stops resisting truth.
YHWH remembers Jacob, Isaac, Abraham, and the land.
The land rests while the people face what they rejected.
Yet even in enemy land, YHWH refuses total rejection or covenant destruction.
The covenant of liberation from Egypt is remembered before the nations.
These are the Sinai instructions set between YHWH and Israel through Moses.

contemplative_note: Leviticus 26 is not a permission slip to blame sufferers. It is communal covenant-warning poetry that moves through blessing, rupture, trauma, land-rest, confession, and the final refusal of abandonment.
